af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Full-stack IoT platform with excellent docs at docs.particle.io. auth_complexity_score=55 due to three token types: user tokens, product tokens, and customer tokens — agents must use product bearer tokens for automation. Device offline handling is critical: function calls fail silently if device is offline. Rate limits are per-token (not per-device), creating fleet-scale gotchas. Retry guidance documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth 2.0 with multiple token scopes (user, product, customer) enabling fine-grained access control. Product tokens limit access to specific product fleets. SOC2 and GDPR compliant. Webhook integrations use Particle-specific auth setup. Secrets managed via API tokens in environment variables.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page available. Stable REST API. Retry guidance documented. Events have short TTL — offline devices may miss published events. Cellular connectivity adds reliability beyond WiFi-only IoT. Fleet-scale rate limit aggregation needs careful management.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Versatile PDF processing API with SDKs in Python, Node, Java, PHP, C#. Simple API key authentication. Large file processing is asynchronous — agents must poll for completion or await status URL. URL-based file input is far more efficient than base64 for large files. Credits consumed even on partial failure — agents should validate output. Rate limits not prominently documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Single API key per account — no scope granularity. GDPR compliance documented. Documents processed through cloud service — data handling policies apply. API key passed as x-api-key header (good practice). No fine-grained access control.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Basic status monitoring. Stable API. Async processing model means timeouts need careful handling. Processing time varies with file size and complexity. Credits consumed on failure creates retry cost risk. OCR quality depends on input scan quality.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Search-augmented LLM API compatible with OpenAI SDK (change base URL and model name). Simple Bearer token authentication. Rate limits not clearly documented — agents may hit limits without clear error guidance. Each web search adds latency and cost. Citation format varies across responses. Model availability changes without clear notice.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. Bearer token authentication, OpenAI-compatible. No fine-grained scope control. GDPR compliant. API key via environment variables recommended. Security posture appropriate for an LLM API service.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page available. OpenAI-compatible interface provides migration stability. Model availability changes can break agent configurations. Search adds latency variability. Retry guidance not documented. High-volume usage can be expensive due to per-query search costs.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. API-first KYC/AML identity verification platform. Simple API key authentication with separate sandbox/production keys. Verification is asynchronous — agents must poll or use webhooks for results. PII data (ID images, SSN) requires careful handling per jurisdiction. Decline reasons may be limited by privacy law. OpenAPI spec and SDKs available.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory for KYC compliance. API key with strict sandbox/production isolation. SOC2 Type II, GDPR, CCPA compliant. Webhook events should be idempotently processed. PII data handling requires compliance with applicable privacy laws. No fine-grained API key scoping — full access per key.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: SOC2 with SLA commitments. Stable versioned API. Retry guidance documented. Async verification with webhook delivery includes retry handling. Duplicate webhook events possible — idempotent processing required. Cursor-based pagination.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Free stock photos and videos API. Simple API key in Authorization header — requires application approval. Attribution to Pexels and photographer legally required for free tier use. CDN download URLs expire — agents should download immediately, not cache URLs. 200 req/hour rate limit hits quickly in content generation pipelines. Photos and videos use separate endpoints.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. Single API key per application — no scope granularity. GDPR compliant. No sensitive data processed — public creative content. API key in Authorization header (good practice). Application approval required helps prevent abuse.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: CDN-backed delivery with good reliability. Stable v1 API. Mature service with backward compatibility. Offset-based pagination. Retry guidance minimal. Free tier continues to be maintained as a core business offer.

af_score_components.notes: Has public MCP server at mcp.tigerdata.com. Provides version-aware PostgreSQL documentation and best-practice skills to AI coding assistants. No authentication required — public server. One-click installs for Cursor, VS Code, Claude. pgvector documentation coming soon. Skills are Timescale-opinionated — may conflict with org-specific conventions. Error handling is basic.

security_score_components.security_notes: Public MCP server with HTTPS. No authentication by design — serves public PostgreSQL documentation. Any client can access — no access control. No sensitive data handled (documentation only). Timescale-maintained server. Appropriate security model for a public docs service.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Depends on mcp.tigerdata.com infrastructure availability — no published SLA. Timescale is a mature company likely maintaining this reliably. Documentation content is stable. Basic retry patterns. Server availability is the primary reliability risk.

af_score_components.notes: Has MCP server. Natural language to SQL for PostgreSQL — read-only by design. Requires DATABASE_URL env var and optional OPENAI_API_KEY for AI-powered SQL generation. Optional bearer token for request authentication. Streaming support for large result sets. Mixed-case table names and composite keys need correct schema introspection. Rate limits not documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: TLS for database connections depends on DATABASE_URL configuration — operator responsibility. Optional bearer token for API auth. Read-only enforcement is a strong security feature. DATABASE_URL via env var is standard practice. OpenAI API key required for full NL capability — store in env. Open source — operator must audit deployment.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted or locally run — no SLA. Early open source project with limited production track record. Streaming support for large result sets is a reliability positive. NL-to-SQL accuracy depends on OpenAI API quality. Retry guidance not documented.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server (no native MCP; agents must use REST API with OpenAPI 3.0 spec). Self-hosted PHP 8.4+ FAQ platform. auth_complexity_score=50 — complex enterprise auth options (LDAP, AD, Entra ID, 2FA, local accounts) make setup non-trivial. No rate limiting documentation. Error handling is basic. Elasticsearch/OpenSearch required for full search performance at scale.

security_score_components.security_notes: Self-hosted deployment — TLS quality depends on operator. Strong auth options: LDAP, Active Directory, Microsoft Entra ID, 2FA. Per-user permission management available. PHP 8.4+ with open source dependencies — regular updates needed. Credential and config management is operator responsibility. No formal security audit.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Self-hosted — no SLA. Long-running mature project (20+ years) with consistent versioning. Migration support between versions. Reliability depends entirely on operator's hosting infrastructure. Elasticsearch integration adds complexity. No built-in HA or clustering.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Premier managed vector database with excellent docs, REST + gRPC APIs, and SDKs in Python, Node, Java, Go. Simple API key authentication (project-scoped). Index creation is async — must poll for READY state. Serverless indexes have eventual consistency — freshly upserted vectors may not appear immediately. Namespace must match between upsert and query. Metadata filtering has size limits (~40KB). Retry guidance documented.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS mandatory. API keys scoped to Pinecone projects. Enterprise RBAC with org-level key management available. SOC2 Type II, GDPR, HIPAA compliant. API key via environment variables. Index and namespace isolation provides data separation. Strong security posture appropriate for production AI applications.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page and SLA. Stable REST and gRPC APIs. Managed service with good versioning. Retry guidance documented. Eventual consistency on serverless indexes documented with clear guidance. Free tier limited to 1 index. gRPC SDK requires separate install. High reliability as core production infrastructure.

af_score_components.notes: No MCP server. Sales-focused CRM with REST API, OpenAPI spec, and SDKs. API token for server-side (simpler for agents), OAuth 2.0 with scopes for user-context apps. Rate limit of 100 req/2s can be hit by agents processing large imports. Custom fields require querying schema for field keys first. Deal stages are pipeline-specific — pipeline ID required before stage operations. No free tier after trial.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. API token + OAuth 2.0 with scopes. GDPR and ISO 27001 certified. API token grants full access (no per-resource scoping), OAuth provides better scope control. Webhook delivery available. Secrets via environment variables. Strong compliance posture for a CRM handling sales data.

reliability_score_components.reliability_notes: Status page available. Stable v1 API with OpenAPI spec. ISO 27001 and GDPR suggest mature operations. Offset-based pagination. Retry guidance not documented. Rate limit (100 req/2s) requires agent throttling for batch operations.
